sections.paragraph:
διϋλίζω (in fig. sense in Pseudo-Archytas [c. 360 b.c.; Stob. III/1, p. 58, 7 H.]. In lit. mng., of wine Plut., Mor. 692d; Diosc. 2, 86; 5, 72; Artem. 4, 48; POxy 413, 154; Am 6:6) filter out, strain out fr. a liquid (the KJV ‘strain at’ is widely considered a misprint [so Goodsp., Relig. in Life 12, ’42/43, 205–10 and Probs. ’45, 38f], but for the view that it is an archaic usage s. OED s.v. ‘strain’, verb, 14e and esp. 21, and CHopf, Rev. of Engl. Studies 20, ’44, 155f; ‘strain at’=‘strain [the liquid] at [seeing]’ a gnat) τὸν κώνωπα a gnat fr. a drink Mt 23:24.—DELG s.v. ὕλη.

Stob Stob , V a.d.—List 5

  1. H. = FHort, s. W-H.—List 1, beg.

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

mng. mng. = meaning(s)

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Diosc Diosc , or Dioscurides, I a.d. physician, also Ps.-Dioscorides after I a.d.—List 5

Artem Artem , onir., II a.d.—List 5

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

  1. fr. = from

KJV KJV = King James version of the Bible—List 6

Goodsp. Goodsp. = EGoodspeed; with no title specified, this abbr. refers to The New Testament: An American Translation—List 6

OED OED = Oxford English Dictionary—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

esp. esp. = especially

Engl. Engl. = English

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 252
